title: Share of women in parliament
subtitle: Percentage of seats in lower or single chamber of the legislature held by women.
caption:
Share of women in parliament

Percentage of seats in lower or single chamber of the legislature held by women.

This choropleth world map displays the share of women in parliament for 2024, representing the percentage of seats in the lower or single chamber of the legislature held by women. The legend uses a sequential color scale ranging from light yellow for 0% to dark purple for 50% and above, with intermediate bands at 0-10%, 10-20%, 20-30%, 30-40%, and 40-50%. Countries with no available data are marked with diagonal hatching, visible in Greenland, Somalia, and parts of the Middle East including Saudi Arabia and Yemen. The darkest purple band, indicating 50% or more female representation, includes Mexico, Rwanda, South Africa, and several European nations such as Spain, France, and Iceland. A cluster of countries in Latin America, including Argentina and Bolivia, along with New Zealand and Namibia, appear in the 40-50% band. Much of Europe, Canada, Australia, and Brazil sit in the 30-40% range, shown in medium-dark purple shades. Large portions of Africa, Asia, and the Middle East fall into the lighter bands: India, China, and Russia appear in the 10-20% range, while countries like Nigeria, Egypt, and Iran sit in the 0-10% band. The United States and Japan are positioned in the 20-30% category. The visual distribution shows higher concentrations of women in parliament across Western Europe, parts of Latin America, and Southern Africa, while lower shares are concentrated across much of Asia, the Middle East, and Eastern Europe.
